info.title: Temps
info.description: An API for managing projects, deployments, and infrastructure resources

servers.description: Base path for all API endpoints

get.summary: Get platform information
Chunk upload options (stub for sentry-cli compatibility).
get.description:
sentry-cli checks this endpoint to determine if chunk-based upload is supported.
We return a response indicating that chunk upload is NOT supported, which forces
sentry-cli to fall back to the standard file-by-file upload.

post.summary: Create a release (stub for sentry-cli compatibility).
post.description:
sentry-cli calls this before uploading files. Since Temps implicitly creates
releases when source maps are uploaded, this is a no-op that returns the
expected response format.

post.summary: Create a release for a specific project (stub for sentry-cli compatibility).
post.description:
sentry-cli calls this endpoint (instead of /organizations/.../releases/) when
both SENTRY_ORG and SENTRY_PROJECT env vars are set. Behaves identically to
the organizations endpoint but validates the project slug.

put.summary: Finalize a release (stub for sentry-cli compatibility).
put.description:
sentry-cli calls `releases finalize` after uploading source maps. This sets
the dateReleased on the release. Since Temps stores source maps independently
of releases, this is a no-op that returns the expected response.

get.summary: List files for a release.
get.description: Returns all source maps stored for a specific release in sentry-cli compatible format.

post.summary: Upload a source map file for a release.
post.description:
Accepts the same multipart format as the Sentry release files API.
The `name` field should be the URL path of the file (e.g., `~/dist/bundle.js.map`).

The route has a 50 MiB body limit applied at the router level (Fix #4).
A per-field size check provides an additional defense-in-depth layer.

413.description: Source map file exceeds the 50 MiB per-field limit

post.summary: Public webhook endpoint. Authenticated via `X-Webhook-Token` header.
post.description:
`POST /api/agents/webhook/{webhook_id}`
Header: `X-Webhook-Token: <secret>`

The `webhook_id` in the URL is a short non-secret identifier (safe to log).
The actual credential is the secret token in the header.

Accepts any JSON body, which is passed as `user_context` to the agent run.

get.summary: List open backup alerts.
get.description:
Returns all alerts that have not yet been resolved, ordered by `opened_at`
descending (newest first). The UI renders these as a banner above the
Backups page content. Alerts are auto-opened by the watcher and
auto-resolved when the triggering condition clears.

**Schedule overdue** — the backup scheduler did not enqueue a job within
the expected window (1 hour past `next_run`). Usually means the scheduler
task is dead or wedged.

**Job stalled** — a `backup_jobs` row has been in `state='pending'` for
more than 1 hour. The runner never claimed the job. Usually means the
runner task is dead or the runner concurrency cap is too low.

post.summary: Run a backup for an external service manually.
post.description:
Enqueues the backup for asynchronous execution via the `BackupRunner`
(ADR-014). Returns `202 Accepted` immediately: pending parent and child
rows are inserted, and a `backup_jobs` row is enqueued for the resolved
engine. Poll `GET /backups/{id}` to observe `pending → running → completed`.

Enqueues the backup for asynchronous execution via the `BackupRunner`

`ControlPlaneEngine`. Poll `GET /backups/{id}` to observe
`pending → running → completed`.

Cancel every non-terminal child backup belonging to a schedule run.
post.description:
Loops over `state IN ('pending','running')` children and flips each via
the same path as the per-backup cancel endpoint. The parent
`schedule_runs.finished_at` is stamped automatically once no live
children remain. Idempotent: cancelling a run with no live children is
a 200 with `cancelled = 0`.

`page_size` defaults to 50 and is capped at 200.

All request fields are optional; only fields that are present in the

post.summary: Immediately fan-out a run for the given schedule (Run Now).
post.description:
Creates one `schedule_runs` row, one control-plane backup job, and one
backup job per supported external service — all in a single transaction.
Returns `202 Accepted` with a [`ScheduleRunResponse`] containing the new
`schedule_run_id` and the list of enqueued jobs. Returns `409 Conflict` if
a run for this schedule is already in flight or if the schedule is disabled.

202.description: Fan-out run enqueued for async execution

409.description: Run already in flight or schedule disabled

get.summary: Paginated run history for a backup schedule (one row per scheduler tick).
get.description:
Returns one [`ScheduleRunSummary`] per scheduler tick, with child backup
counts aggregated in a single SQL round-trip. Legacy `backups` rows (pre-
fan-out) are surfaced as synthetic single-job runs so history does not
disappear. Ordered by `started_at DESC` (newest first).

Use `GET /backups/schedule-runs/{run_id}/jobs` to drill into a single run.

post.summary: Cancel a single in-flight backup.
post.description:
Flips the parent `backups` row + its latest `backup_jobs` row to
`failed` with reason `"cancelled by user <uid>"`. The in-process
`CancellationToken` is observed on the next heartbeat tick (≤5s), so the
engine exits cleanly and rollback reaps the sidecar. Idempotent: cancelling
an already-terminal backup is a 200 with `cancelled = 0`.

get.summary: List the external-service child backups that belong to a parent backup.
get.description:
Each entry in `children` corresponds to one `external_service_backups` row,
joined with `external_services` so the caller receives the service name and
type without a second request.

Returns an empty `{ "children": [] }` — **not 404** — when the parent
backup exists but has no children (e.g. control-plane backups).
Returns 404 when the parent backup itself does not exist.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Integer row id of the parent backup

post.summary: Create a new domain
post.description:
Creates a new domain and automatically requests a Let's Encrypt challenge.
You can specify the challenge type (HTTP-01 or DNS-01) in the request.

- **HTTP-01**: Validates domain ownership by placing a file on your web server at `/.well-known/acme-challenge/`
- **DNS-01**: Validates domain ownership by adding a TXT record to your DNS (required for wildcard domains)

get.summary: Get domain details by hostname
Get ACME order for a domain

post.summary: Create or recreate ACME order for a domain
post.description:
Creates a new ACME order with Let's Encrypt for the specified domain.
If an order already exists, you should cancel it first using the cancel-order endpoint.
Returns the challenge details that need to be fulfilled (DNS record or HTTP token).

delete.summary: Cancel ACME order for a domain
delete.description:
Cancels the current ACME order for a domain and clears all challenge data.
This allows you to start over with a new order if the previous one failed or got stuck.

post.summary: Finalize ACME order for a domain
post.description:
Finalizes the ACME order by completing the challenge validation and requesting the certificate.
This should be called after the challenge has been set up (DNS record added or HTTP token served).

post.summary: Setup DNS challenge records automatically using a DNS provider
post.description:
This endpoint automatically creates the required DNS TXT records for ACME DNS-01 challenge
validation using a configured DNS provider. The domain must have an active DNS challenge
pending (created via POST /domains/{id}/order with dns-01 challenge type).

This is similar to how email domain DNS records are auto-provisioned.

400.description: Bad request - DNS provider not configured or no challenge pending

get.summary: Per-member health for a Postgres HA cluster.
get.description:
Reads pg_auto_failover's `pgautofailover.node` table from the cluster's
monitor (TLS, autoctl_node) and joins each member with its
`pg_stat_replication` row from the current primary. Returns one row per
data member with role/state, sync state, and replay lag.

Returns `200` with `monitor_error` set when the monitor is briefly
unreachable (UI surfaces it as a banner above the table); the table
itself is empty in that case. Returns `400` for non-cluster services.

post.summary: Run a health check for one service right now
post.description:
Triggers the same engine-specific probe as the background monitor, writes
a history row, updates the denormalized fields on `external_services`, and
fires alerts on the Nth consecutive failure (so consecutive-failure state
stays honest). Returns the fresh snapshot the UI can display immediately.

503.description: Health monitor not running on this node

get.summary: Persisted health status for an external service
get.description:
Returns the latest health probe result recorded by
`ExternalServiceHealthMonitor`, plus recent check history for sparklines
and a 24-hour uptime percentage. Safe to poll from the UI every 30s.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max number of recent checks (default 50, max 200)

post.summary: Begin adding a single new member to a running cluster.
post.description:
Currently only `replica` members can be added at runtime. The
response is **202 Accepted** as soon as the validation passes and
the placeholder `service_members` row is inserted. The actual
container provisioning + DNS registration runs in the background;
poll `GET /external-services/{id}/members/{member_id}` to watch
`provisioning_step` advance through the phases.

400.description: Validation failed (wrong topology, status, or role)

get.summary: Get a single cluster member's current state.
get.description:
Used by the add-member page to poll the row every second while the
background provisioning task walks through its phases. The
`provisioning_step` field advances through `inserting_row` →
`provisioning_container` → `registering_dns` → `done` (or `failed`
with `provisioning_error` set).

delete.summary: Remove a single member from a running cluster.
delete.description:
Refuses to remove the monitor (singleton), the current primary
(failover first), or any member if the cluster would drop below the
2-data-member quorum required for HA. Stops + removes the container,
deletes the row, and drops the Tier-2 DNS record.

400.description: Validation failed (monitor, primary, or quorum violation)

post.summary:
Promote a replica to primary by triggering a pg_auto_failover
failover. The monitor demotes the current primary and the chosen
replica transitions to primary; the role reconciler then refreshes
the role-aliased VIPs (≤30s).

400.description: Validation failed (monitor, already primary, not running, etc.)

patch.summary: Update a service's resource limits (memory, CPU caps).
patch.description:
Persists the new caps to the encrypted config AND live-applies them
via Docker's update API. Memory and CPU can be hot-changed without a
restart on running containers; stopped containers also accept the
update and pick up the new caps on next start.

Pass `null` (or omit) any field to leave it unlimited. A request where
every field is `null` removes any existing limits.

The response includes a per-container `applied[]` list so the caller
can tell which members got the update and which were skipped (e.g.,
container not yet created, or `docker update` rejected because the
new memory cap is below current usage).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: External service id (source for the restore)

post.summary:
Write a file into the sandbox. Accepts two body shapes — the SDK
picks one based on `Content-Type`:
post.description:
- **`application/json`** (temps-native): `{path, contents_b64, mode}`
  — one file, base64-encoded.
- **`application/gzip`** (`@vercel/sandbox`): a gzipped tarball of
  one-or-more entries, with the target extract dir carried in the
  `x-cwd` header. The SDK's `writeFile` and `writeFiles` both post
  here; they differ only in how many entries the tarball contains.

Why merge them on one route: the SDK is hardcoded to
`POST /fs/write`, so splitting tar uploads onto a separate path would
force us to break SDK compat. Instead we dispatch on Content-Type,
preserve JSON for native callers, and add tar for SDK callers.

post.summary:
Batch-write multiple files in a single request. Mirrors
`@vercel/sandbox` `writeFiles()`. Semantics are fail-fast: if any
file errors, previously-written entries are left in place and the
error describes which file broke.

oneOf.description:
Build-time resource limits applied on the control plane to prevent
`docker build` from saturating host CPU/RAM. Worker nodes are
intentionally NOT subject to these limits (each worker is dedicated
hardware that already has its own per-host headroom).

insecure_tls.description:
Skip TLS certificate verification on outbound HTTP clients built by the
server (deployer, agent, remote service client). Strictly opt-in for
operators running self-signed control plane / worker certs on a trusted
internal network. Worker→control-plane traffic that traverses the public
internet must keep this `false` — otherwise a MitM steals the join token.

internal_url.description:
URL that service containers use to reach the Temps API from *inside*
the Docker network (OTLP metrics ingest, agent callbacks, etc.). On
Docker Desktop this defaults to `http://host.docker.internal:<console_port>`;
on Linux it requires the `host.docker.internal:host-gateway` host
mapping (which Temps adds to provisioned containers). Distinct from
`external_url`, which is the public-facing address.

oneOf.description:
Metrics observability settings. Controls the MetricsStore backend,
scrape interval, and tiered retention windows.

default.content_security_policy: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; img-src 'self' data: https:; font-src 'self' data:; connect-src 'self'; frame-ancestors 'self'

setup_complete.description:
Set to `true` by `temps setup` (all modes) once initial configuration
has been applied. The web onboarding wizard reads this from the server
and skips itself when true, preventing the "Configure Base Domain" wall
from appearing on installs that were already configured via the CLI.

AppSettingsResponse.description: Safe response for application settings that masks sensitive fields

effective_metrics_store.description:
The storage backend the runtime is **actually** using for metrics,
after reconciling the `monitoring.store` toggle with the server's
`TEMPS_CLICKHOUSE_*` configuration. When `monitoring.store` is
`click_house` but those env vars are not fully set, the runtime falls
back to TimescaleDB — in that case this reports `timescale_db` even
though `monitoring.store` says `click_house`. The UI shows this as the
effective backend and warns when it diverges from the configured store.

setup_complete.description:
Whether `temps setup` has been run at least once. The web onboarding
wizard checks this field on load and skips itself when true.

AttachScheduleServicesRequest.description:
Body for `POST /api/backups/schedules/{id}/services` — attach external
services to a backup schedule. Idempotent.

service_ids.description: External service ids to attach. Duplicates are de-duplicated server-side.

inserted.description:
Number of rows actually inserted (excludes rows skipped by
`ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`).

total_attached.description: Total number of services now attached to the schedule.

AuditLogIpInfo.description: IP address information in audit log

AuditLogResponse.description: Response type for audit log entries

id.description: Unique identifier for the audit log entry

oneOf.description: IP address details

operation_type.description: The type of action that was performed

oneOf.description: User details who performed the action

user_id.description: The user who performed the action

AuthFlavorDto.description:
One auth flavor surfaced to the UI. Mirrors `AuthFlavor` in the catalog
but without the seed-path / env-var fields the frontend doesn't need
(those are server-side only — exposing them just bloats the response).

env_var.description:
For `api_key` format: the env var name that will be set inside the
sandbox. Useful for showing the user "we'll set OPENAI_API_KEY" so
they know what their key controls.

format.description:
`api_key`, `oauth_token`, or `config_file` — drives which input UI
the settings page renders (single-line vs. multi-line textarea).

AvailableContainerInfo.description: Available Docker container that can be imported as a service

exposed_ports.description: Exposed ports (e.g., [5432] for PostgreSQL, [6379] for Redis)

is_running.description: Whether the container is currently running

AvailablePermissions.description: Response containing all available permissions for frontend validation

permissions.description: All available permissions in the system

BackupAlertListResponse.description: Response body for the list-backup-alerts endpoint.

alerts.description: All currently open (unresolved) alerts, newest first.

BackupAlertResponse.description:
A single open backup alert surfaced in the UI banner.

Alerts are auto-opened by the watcher and auto-resolved when the triggering
condition clears. No manual dismiss is required or supported.

The optional `schedule_s3_source_id` field is included so the UI can
deep-link an `overdue_schedule` alert to the S3 source detail page that
hosts the schedule. `stalled_job` alerts no longer carry a deep-link
target — the alert message text contains the backup id for display.

id.description: Database id of the alert row.

message.description: Human-readable description of the alert condition.

opened_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when the alert was opened.

schedule_name.description: Human-readable name of the linked schedule, if applicable.

schedule_s3_source_id.description:
FK to `backup_schedules.s3_source_id`. The UI uses this to deep-link
the alert to the S3 source detail page that hosts the schedule.
Set for `overdue_schedule` alerts.

attempts.description:
How many times this job has been claimed and run. `null` for legacy
backups with no `backup_jobs` row.

current_step.description:
Name of the engine step currently executing (e.g., `"walg_push"`).
`null` when no `backup_jobs` row exists for this backup (legacy rows
pre-dating ADR-014), or when the job has not yet completed its first step.

oneOf.description:
External service that owns this backup (Redis, Postgres, etc.).
`null` for control-plane backups (the Temps server's own database).

live_size_bytes.description:
Best-effort partial size while a backup is still running, computed
by listing the S3 prefix. Null when the backup is finished
(`size_bytes` is authoritative in that case).

max_attempts.description:
Maximum attempts before the job is permanently failed. `null` for
legacy backups.

max_runtime_secs.description:
Resolved wall-clock timeout for this backup job (seconds). `null` for
legacy backups. Derived from the three-tier resolution order:
caller override → schedule override → engine default.

size_bytes.description: Final size of the backup once completed. Null while running.

include_control_plane.description:
When `true`, every run also produces a `control_plane` backup
(Temps's own Postgres). When `false`, only the external service
fan-out happens.

max_runtime_secs.description:
Per-schedule wall-clock timeout override for backup jobs (seconds).
`null` means the engine-family default is used. See
`temps_backup_core::timeouts::default_max_runtime_secs`.

target_all_services.description:
When `true`, the schedule auto-includes every external service on
the host (and any future ones). When `false`, the schedule only
targets services attached via `backup_schedule_services`.

url.description: URL path to access the blob

enabled.description: Whether the Blob service is enabled

BuildConfiguration.description: Build configuration (for building images from source)

BuildLimitsSettings.description:
Control-plane build resource limits.

Caps how many builds run concurrently AND how much CPU/memory each build
is allowed to consume. A single global semaphore in the deployer crate
gates every `DockerRuntime::build_image` call to `max_concurrent`. When
the semaphore is full, additional builds queue and wait — they do not
fail. Per-build CPU/memory caps are forwarded to Docker via
`BuildImageOptions { memory, cpuquota, cpuperiod }`.

`cpu_limit_cores = 0.0` or `memory_limit_mb = 0` means "no explicit cap"
— fall back to the legacy 50%-of-host heuristic for backwards
compatibility with operators who never visit the settings page.

cpu_limit_cores.description:
CPU cores allowed per build (float, e.g. 2.0 = 2 cores, 0.5 = half
a core). 0 means "use the legacy 50%-of-host default".

max_concurrent.description:
Maximum number of `docker build` operations allowed to run at the
same time on the control plane. Additional builds queue. Min 1.

memory_limit_mb.description:
Memory allowed per build, in megabytes. 0 means "use the legacy
50%-of-host default". Docker enforces this as a hard cap — builds
that exceed it OOM-kill.

cancelled.description:
Number of rows that were actually flipped to `failed`. `0` is a valid
success and means the backup was already terminal — the call is
idempotent.

ChallengeConfig.description:
Challenge configuration (future feature)
For CAPTCHA, JS challenges, proof-of-work, etc.

oneOf.description: Error details if validation failed

validated.description: When the challenge was validated (if successful)

mfa_code.description: TOTP code (or recovery code). Required iff the user has MFA enabled.

revoke_other_sessions.description:
When true, every session OTHER than the one making this request is
revoked. Defaults to false; the UI surfaces this as a checkbox.

allOf.description: Tolerates extra SDK fields (stream_options, logprobs, etc.)

ChatCompletionRequest.description:
OpenAI-compatible chat completion request.
Uses `deny_unknown_fields = false` (serde default) so that SDK-specific
fields like `stream_options`, `logprobs`, `top_logprobs`, `logit_bias`,
`parallel_tool_calls`, etc. are silently accepted without breaking.

ChildBackupEntryResponse.description:
A single child backup entry in the `GET /backups/{id}/children` response.

Each entry corresponds to one `external_service_backups` row joined with
`external_services`, providing service metadata without a second request.

finished_at.description: When the child backup finished, if known.

s3_location.description: Object key or `s3://` URL where the backup data lives.

service_name.description: Human-readable name of the external service (e.g. "redis-prod").

size_bytes.description: Size of the child backup in bytes, if available.

ChildBackupListResponse.description:
Response body for `GET /backups/{id}/children`.

Returns an empty `children` list (not 404) when the parent backup has no
child records (e.g. control-plane backups).

children.description: Zero or more child backup entries ordered by `external_service_backups.id` ASC.

oneOf.description: Still waiting on the user to approve in the browser.
CLI is polling faster than the server-suggested interval.
User denied the request in the browser.
The session has expired without approval.

The session was approved; this is the only response that carries
the API key. The key is returned exactly once and then cleared
from the session row.

client_name.description:
Friendly hostname / client identifier shown in the browser approval
screen. Sanitized before display.

device_code.description: Opaque secret the CLI polls with. Never display to a human.

user_code.description: Short human-readable code the user types into the browser.

verification_uri.description:
Base verification URL — the CLI may display this when the
pre-filled URL is too long to be useful.

checked_at.description: ISO-8601 wall-clock when the report was generated.

monitor_error.description: Set when the monitor itself was unreachable. UI shows a banner.

ClusterMemberHealthResponse.description: One row in the cluster Members table — see `GET /external-services/{id}/cluster-health`.

goal_state.description:
What the monitor *wants* the node to be. Differs from
`reported_state` mid-transition (failover, demotion, etc.).

health.description:
pg_auto_failover liveness signal: `1` healthy, `0` unknown
(no recent report), `-1` unhealthy.

reported_state.description: What the node *last told the monitor* it was. Stale during outages.

seconds_since_report.description: Wall-clock seconds since the node last reported in.

ClusterMemberRequest.description: Request spec for a single cluster member.

node_id.description: Target worker node ID. Omit or null to run on the control plane.

args.description: Arguments to pass to the binary. Defaults to empty.

command.description:
Binary name (argv[0]) — e.g. `"ls"`, `"node"`. The SDK sends this
separately from `args`.

sudo.description:
When true, the SDK runs the command privileged. We ignore it today
— the underlying provider always runs as the sandbox's own user.

wait.description:
When true, the response is an `application/x-ndjson` stream where
the first line is the running-command envelope and the second line
is the finished-command envelope with `exitCode`.

CmdInner.description:
Inner `command` object — matches the SDK's zod validator exactly.
`exitCode` is `null` until the command terminates; `startedAt` is Unix
epoch milliseconds.

CmdKillBody.description:
SDK-shaped kill body. The SDK sends `{signal: AbortSignal}` but only
uses the signal for HTTP request abortion client-side; there's no
signal name on the wire.

force.description: Optional: when true, SIGKILL instead of SIGTERM.

health_message.description: Human-readable reason when health_status is "unhealthy"; null otherwise.

synced_repository_count.description:
Running count of repositories persisted by the current (or most
recent) sync. Resets to 0 when a new sync begins; useful for showing
live progress on large syncs.

ConsoleEventPayload.description:
Payload for server-side event ingestion via the console API.

The app backend reads the encrypted `_temps_visitor_id` and `_temps_sid`
cookie values from the user's request and forwards them here.
Temps decrypts them server-side to resolve visitor/session identity.

deployment_id.description: Deployment ID to attribute the event to

environment_id.description: Environment ID to attribute the event to

session_id.description: Encrypted `_temps_sid` cookie value from the user's browser

visitor_id.description: Encrypted `_temps_visitor_id` cookie value from the user's browser

cpu_limit_cores.description: CPU limit in whole cores (e.g. 1.0). None when no limit is configured.

oom_killed.description: True when Docker's OOM killer terminated the container.

started_at.description: When the container's main process most recently started.

cpu_limit_cores.description: CPU limit in whole cores (e.g. 1.0). None when no limit is configured.

error_message.description: Free-form error string from Docker's container state on exit.

exit_code.description: Process exit code reported by Docker. None while still running.

exit_reason.description:
Human-readable reason the container exited (e.g. "OOMKilled",
"Killed by SIGKILL (exit code 137)", "Exit code 1"). None while running.

finished_at.description: When the container exited (Docker's FinishedAt). None while running.

node_name.description: Node name where this container is running. None for local (single-node) deployments.

oom_killed.description: True when Docker's OOM killer terminated the container.

restart_count.description:
Container restart count from Docker. The UI shows a chip when this is
> 0 so a crash loop is visible without opening detail.

service_name.description: Compose service name (e.g. "web", "redis"). None for single-container deployments.

started_at.description:
When the container's main process most recently started. The UI uses
this for the uptime label so the count resets when a container is
restarted in place. None for containers that never started.

ContainerInventoryItem.description: A container reported by the agent during heartbeat reconciliation.

ContainerLogSettings.description:
Docker container log rotation settings
Controls the `--log-opt max-size` and `--log-opt max-file` for containers

max_file.description: Maximum number of rotated log files to keep (e.g., 3 means up to 3 x max_size total)

max_size.description:
Maximum size of each log file (e.g., "50m", "100m", "1g")
Docker default is unlimited; we default to "50m" to prevent disk exhaustion

service_max_file.description: Maximum rotated log files for external service containers

service_max_size.description:
Maximum size for external service container logs (postgres, redis, etc.)
Defaults to "20m" since services are typically less verbose than app containers

container_name.description: Optional container name to get logs from (if deployment has multiple containers)

follow.description: Follow log output in real-time (default: true for backward compatibility)

timestamps.description: Include timestamps in log output (default: false)

cpu_limit_cores.description: CPU limit in whole cores (e.g. 1.0). None = no limit.

cpu_percent.description:
CPU usage as a multi-core percentage (Docker convention: 200 = 2 cores
fully pinned). Divide by 100 to get cores used.

memory_percent.description: Memory usage percentage (0-100) if limit is set

can_contain_containers.description: Can this container hold other containers?

can_contain_entities.description: Can this container hold entities (tables, collections, etc.)?

child_container_type.description: Type of child containers (if can_contain_containers is true)

container_type.description: Container type (database, schema, keyspace, bucket, etc.)

entity_count_hint.description: Hint for UI on expected entity count (small = sidebar, large = pagination)

entity_type_label.description: Label for entity type (if can_contain_entities is true)

ContainerRuntimeInfo.description:
Snapshot of a container's lifecycle state from `docker inspect`.
`restart_count` and `oom_killed` are the load-bearing fields when
diagnosing crash loops — the kernel OOM killer never reaches the
application's logs, so seeing `oom_killed=true` is the only signal
that a memory limit was the cause.

container_id.description:
Container Docker id, when present. None = container does not exist
(was never created or was removed externally).

container_name.description: Stable name of the Docker container (e.g. `postgres-mydb`).

exit_code.description: Last container exit code, when known. Non-zero = unclean stop.

finished_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the most recent termination, when known.

oom_killed.description:
True when the container's last termination was caused by the
kernel OOM killer. Set if the user enabled hard memory limits
and the working set exceeded them.

resource_limits.description:
Currently-applied resource limits read off the container's
`HostConfig`. Compare this against the user-configured limits to
detect drift (an old container that never picked up new caps).

restart_count.description:
Total restarts since the container was created. Useful for
detecting crash loops — a steady stream means something is killing
the container repeatedly (frequently OOM).

started_at.description:
ISO-8601 timestamp of when the container last started. None when
it has never started (i.e. created but never run).

status.description:
Bollard container state ("running", "exited", "dead", etc.). None
when the container does not exist.

ContainerStatsSample.description:
Live resource usage sample for a single container.

`cpu_percent` is computed by Docker's standard formula:
  ((cpu_delta / system_delta) * online_cpus) * 100
`memory_percent` is `(memory_usage / memory_limit) * 100` — when no
memory limit is set the limit reported by Docker is the host's total
RAM, so a 5% reading means "5% of host RAM", not "5% of allocated".

cpu_percent.description:
CPU usage as a percentage. `None` when the container is not running
(Docker returns no usable counters).

memory_limit_bytes.description: Memory limit in bytes (host RAM if no limit set).

memory_percent.description: Memory usage as a percentage of `memory_limit_bytes`.

online_cpus.description:
Number of cores Docker observed at sample time. Used by the UI
to label "x/y cores" instead of just a percent.

is_match.description: Whether this line matched the original search

lines.description: Number of context lines before and after (default: 25)

ConversationSummary.description: A conversation summary grouping related AI invocations.

projectId.description: Project ID (required for API key/session auth, optional for deployment tokens)

cooldown_minutes.description: Minimum minutes between notifications for same rule+group

environment_filter.description: Optional environment ID to filter alerts

notification_priority.description: Notification priority: Low, Normal, High, Critical

trigger_type.description: Trigger type: new_issue, regression, frequency, new_user, user_count, status_change

include_control_plane.description:
When `true` (default), every run also produces a `control_plane`
backup of Temps's own database. Operators who use Temps purely as
a backup orchestrator for external DBs can set this to `false` to
keep the run history focused on those services.

max_runtime_secs.description:
Optional wall-clock timeout override for jobs created by this schedule
(seconds). When set, overrides the engine-family default. `null` means
"use engine default." The per-job `max_runtime_secs` in
`EnqueueJobParams` can still override this for ad-hoc triggers.

s3_source_id.description: Optional S3 source. If omitted, the current default S3 source is used.

target_all_services.description:
When `true` (default), the schedule backs up every external service
on the host — including databases created in the future. When
`false`, the schedule backs up only the services explicitly attached
via `POST /backups/schedules/{id}/services`. Omit to use the default.

you only have SMTP creds and want to import an already-set-up domain.

When true the variable is treated as write-only: never returned in
plaintext from the API, masked in the UI, and updates that omit the

vars cannot be demoted back to regular vars.

Defaults false. Set to true only for IdPs where an admin

`trust_idp_email` field on `oidc_providers::Model` for the

credentials.description: Platform credentials (required for cloud platforms like Vercel, Railway)

repository_id.description:
Optional repository ID to associate with the import
If provided, preset will be detected from the repository

can_execute.description: Whether the plan can be executed

automatic_deploy.description: Enable automatic deployment on push (defaults to true)

exposed_port.description:
Port exposed by the container (fallback when image has no EXPOSE directive)

Priority order for port resolution:
1. Image EXPOSE directive (auto-detected from built image)
2. Environment-level exposed_port (overrides this value per environment)
3. This project-level exposed_port (fallback)
4. Default: 3000

Only set this if your image doesn't use EXPOSE directive.

oneOf.description:
Preset-specific configuration

Different presets accept different configuration options:
- **Dockerfile preset**: Accepts `DockerfilePresetConfig` with `dockerfile_path` and `build_context`
- **Nixpacks preset**: Uses `nixpacks.toml` file for configuration (no params needed)
- **Static presets** (Vite, Next.js, etc.): Accept `StaticPresetConfig` with build commands and output dir

Example for Dockerfile preset:
```json
{
  "dockerfilePath": "docker/Dockerfile",
  "buildContext": "./api"
}
```

source_type.description:
Source type for deployments

Determines how the project is deployed:
- **git** (default): Traditional Git-based deployments - source code is pulled, built, and deployed
- **docker_image**: Deploy pre-built Docker images from external registries (DockerHub, GHCR, etc.)
- **static_files**: Deploy pre-built static files uploaded as tar.gz or zip bundles

For `docker_image` and `static_files` source types, `repo_name` and `repo_owner` are optional.

CreateProjectSecretRequest.description:
Request to create a new project secret.

Project secrets are mounted into the container as files under
`/run/secrets/<KEY>` (mode 0400, tmpfs) instead of as environment variables.
Values are always encrypted at rest and never returned in plaintext from
the API after create. Distinct from agent secrets (global `/settings/secrets`).

include_in_preview.description: Include this secret in preview environments.

key.description:
Identifier for the secret. Becomes the filename at `/run/secrets/<KEY>`.
Must start with a letter or underscore and contain only A-Z, a-z, 0-9, _.

route_type.description:
Route type: "http" (default) matches on HTTP Host header,
"tls" matches on TLS SNI hostname for TCP passthrough

endpoint.description: Optional endpoint URL for S3-compatible services like MinIO

force_path_style.description: Whether to use path-style addressing (default: true)

is_default.description:
When true, make this the default source (will swap out any existing default).
The very first S3 source is always created as default regardless of this flag.

env.description: Extra env vars baked into the container on create.

image.description: Docker image override. `null` uses the platform default.

Ports the sandbox will listen on. Each port becomes a `routes[]`
entry in the create/get response so `@vercel/sandbox`'s
`sandbox.domain(port)` can resolve it client-side without an
extra round-trip.

preview_password.description:
Optional preview-URL password. When set, every preview URL served
for this sandbox is gated behind a login form. 8–256 characters.
Omit to leave preview URLs open (the sandbox ID remains the only
gate). The plaintext is never returned; only the last-4 hint is
surfaced in `SandboxResponse.preview_password_hint`.

`@vercel/sandbox`'s nested resources object. When present, its
`memory` / `vcpus` populate `memory_limit_mb` / `cpu_limit` if those
weren't sent directly.

Optional initial content to seed into the work dir. Clones a
repo or extracts a tarball after the sandbox is created.

Idle timeout as sent by `@vercel/sandbox` (milliseconds). Converted
to seconds when `timeout_secs` is absent.

A Postgres instance can host many databases (some unrelated to this

"Databases" breakdown table instead of one collapsed number.

Latest value of each requested metric for this database
(e.g. `{"pg.database_size_bytes": 7943871, "pg.cache_hit_ratio": 0.99}`).

Tag to apply to the imported image (e.g., "myapp:v1.0")
If not provided, a unique tag will be generated

DeploymentConfig.description:
Deployment configuration shared between projects and environments

This configuration can be set at the project level (as defaults) and
overridden at the environment level for specific deployments.

Note: Environment variables are managed separately and are not part of this config.

antiAffinity.description:
Anti-affinity: spread replicas across different nodes.

When enabled, the scheduler avoids placing two replicas of the same
environment on the same node. If there are fewer eligible nodes than
replicas, remaining replicas wrap around (best-effort spreading).

Label selector for node-based scheduling. Replicas are only deployed to

- **Same key, array value** → OR: node must match any value
- **Different keys** → AND: node must satisfy all keys

Example: `{"region": ["us", "asia"], "gpu": "true"}`
→ (region=us OR region=asia) AND gpu=true

Applied after `target_nodes` filtering (they stack).

Optional list of node IDs to deploy to. When set, replicas are distributed

across all active nodes (or deploys locally if no nodes exist).

  [`NormalizedEventType::MrrRealized`] events instead. Correct for

* `UseSubscription`: trust whatever MRR the subscription parser
  returns (0 for metered). Legacy behavior.
* `Ignore`: drop metered subscriptions from MRR entirely.

A single step in the migration execution plan.

Steps are presented to the user before execution so they know exactly
what will happen. During execution, each step runs in order and reports
its outcome before proceeding to the next.

data_implications.description: Data implications — what could go wrong or what the user needs to know

description.description: Detailed description of what this step does

post_conditions.description: Things the user should verify AFTER this step completes

pre_conditions.description: Things the user should verify BEFORE this step runs

resource_type.description: What kind of resource this step creates/modifies

reversible.description: Whether this step is reversible (can be cleaned up on failure)

skippable.description: Whether this step can be skipped by the user

skipped.description: Whether the user has chosen to skip this step (set during review)

MigrationSummary.description: Human-readable summary of the entire migration plan

critical_warnings.description:
Critical warnings that must be acknowledged before proceeding.
These are the most important things the user needs to know.

headline.description: One-line summary (e.g., "Migrate 'my-app' from Vercel with 1 database, 2 domains")

manual_actions_required.description: Manual actions the user must perform (before or after migration)

overall_risk.description: Overall risk assessment for the migration

unsupported_features.description: Features from the source platform that cannot be migrated

is_b2c.description: Whether the email provider is a B2C (consumer) email provider

is_disposable.description: Whether the email is from a disposable email provider

is_role_account.description: Whether the email is a role-based account (e.g., admin@, info@)

ModelPricing.description:
Pricing for a single model, all values in USD per 1M tokens.
Fields are optional because not every provider supports every pricing tier.

batch_input_per_million.description: Batch API input cost per 1M tokens (if provider offers batch pricing)

batch_output_per_million.description: Batch API output cost per 1M tokens

cache_hit_per_million.description: Cache hit / refresh cost per 1M tokens

cache_write_5m_per_million.description: 5-minute cache write cost per 1M tokens (Anthropic-style prompt caching)

input_per_million.description: Base input token cost per 1M tokens

MonitoringSettings.description:
Global metrics observability configuration.

Controls whether the MetricsScraper and AlertEvaluator background tasks
are active, which storage backend they write to, and how long data is kept
at each retention tier.

clickhouse_url.description:
ClickHouse DSN, required only when `store = "click_house"`.
Example: `"http://localhost:8123"`.

enabled.description:
Enable or disable all metrics collection (scraping + alerting).
Defaults to `false` so new installs don't write to TimescaleDB until
an operator explicitly enables the feature.

retention_daily_years.description: How many years of daily-aggregate data to keep (converted to days internally).

retention_hourly_days.description: How many days of hourly-aggregate data to keep.

retention_raw_days.description: How many days of raw (30 s resolution) metric data to keep.

scrape_interval_secs.description:
How often the MetricsScraper collects data from all sources, in seconds.
Minimum effective value is 10 s; values below that are clamped at runtime.

oneOf.description: Storage backend for metric data.

MonitoringSettingsMasked.description:
Monitoring settings with the ClickHouse DSN masked.

`clickhouse_url` can embed credentials (`http://user:pass@host`), so it is
reported only as a boolean (`clickhouse_url_set`) rather than echoed back —
consistent with how the DNS API key and Docker registry password are masked.

clickhouse_url_set.description:
True when a ClickHouse DSN is configured. The DSN itself is never
returned over HTTP because it may contain credentials.

join_token_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the join token (never store plaintext)

private_address.description:
Private/WireGuard IP address of the control plane node.
Used by remote worker nodes to reach services (databases, etc.) running on the control plane.
Set via `--private-address` or `TEMPS_PRIVATE_ADDRESS`.

error.description: Error message if MX lookup failed

records.description: List of MX records for the domain

NavEntry.description: A navigation entry that the plugin contributes to the Temps UI.

order.description: Sort order within the section (lower = higher in list)

section.description: Which sidebar section this entry belongs to

NavSection.description: Where the plugin's nav entry appears in the Temps UI sidebar.

NixpacksPresetConfig.description:
Configuration for Nixpacks preset
Nixpacks auto-detects your application and uses nixpacks.toml for configuration
No additional parameters needed - configuration is expressed in nixpacks.toml file

NodeContainerResponse.description: A container running on a specific node, enriched with project/environment context.

capacity.description: Resource capacity/usage metrics from the latest heartbeat

ObservabilityEvent.description:
Discriminated union of every row that can appear in the Observe list.

Serializes to `{ "type": "request" | "span" | ... , ...rest }` so the UI
can switch on `event.type` without ambiguity.

**No `Log` variant**: runtime stdout/stderr lines live on a dedicated
Logs page rather than Observe. Logs are too high-volume to interleave
with business signals (requests, errors, revenue) without dominating
the timeline, and they have their own retention/storage constraints
(TimescaleDB hypertable + chunked file/S3 store) that don't compose
with the merge service's per-kind LIMIT strategy.

client_secret.description: Always masked — the secret is never returned after creation.

trust_idp_email.description:
When true, the resolver skips the `email_verified` claim gate
during SSO login. Only safe for IdPs where an admin controls
user provisioning — see `oidc_providers::Model::trust_idp_email`.

slug.description:
Stable opaque slug — use this as the path parameter when initiating
OIDC login (`/auth/oidc/login/{slug}`). The integer database ID is
intentionally omitted from this public endpoint to prevent provider
enumeration.

template.description:
The template the provider was created from — e.g. `keycloak`,
`okta`, `auth0`, `google`, `azure-ad`, or `generic`. Surfaced on
the public login endpoint so the unauthenticated login page can
render the right brand logo on the "Sign in with X" button.
Never sensitive — the template name is part of the provider's
public identity, not configuration.

OidcProviderUserResponse.description:
A user that has logged in via a given OIDC provider. Used by the
admin "Users for provider" panel — the `oidc_subject` is the
IdP-side identifier we matched on, useful when diagnosing why a
user can or can't log in.

PageActivityBucket.description: Time bucket data point for page activity graph

avg_time_seconds.description: Average time on page in seconds

page_views.description: Number of page views in this bucket

visitors.description: Number of unique visitors in this bucket

PageCountryStats.description: Geographic distribution of visitors for a page

page_views.description: Number of page views from this country

visitors.description: Number of unique visitors from this country

PageFlowEntry.description: A single page with its entry/exit/bounce statistics

avg_time_on_page.description: Average time spent on this page in seconds

bounce_count.description: Number of times visitors bounced on this page

bounce_rate.description: Bounce rate: bounce_count / entry_count (only meaningful for entry pages)

entry_count.description: Number of times this page was the entry page of a session

exit_count.description: Number of times this page was the exit page of a session

total_views.description: Total page views for this page

PageFlowQuery.description: Query parameters for page flow analytics

limit.description: Maximum number of entry/exit pages to return (default: 20)

transitions_limit.description: Maximum number of transitions to return (default: 50)

drop_off_points.description: Top drop-off points (highest exit rates with meaningful traffic)

top_entry_pages.description: Top entry pages (where visitors land), sorted by entry_count DESC

top_exit_pages.description: Top exit pages (where visitors leave), sorted by exit_count DESC

total_pages.description: Total unique pages seen in the period

PageHourlySessionsQuery.description: Query parameters for page hourly sessions endpoint

PagePathDetailQuery.description: Query parameters for page path detail analytics

bucket_interval.description: Bucket interval for time series: 'hour', 'day', 'week', 'month' (default: auto)

page_path.description: The specific page path to get details for (URL-encoded)

PagePathDetailResponse.description: Detailed analytics response for a specific page path

activity_over_time.description: Time series data for activity graph

avg_time_on_page.description: Average time on page in seconds

bucket_interval.description: Bucket interval used for time series ('hour', 'day', etc.)

entry_rate.description: Entry rate - percentage of sessions that started on this page

exit_rate.description: Exit rate - percentage of sessions that ended on this page

total_page_views.description: Total page views in the date range

unique_visitors.description: Total unique visitors to this page in the date range

PagePathVisitorsQuery.description: Query parameters for page path visitors

page_path.description: The specific page path to get visitors for

PagePathVisitorsResponse.description: Response for page path visitors endpoint

total_count.description: Total number of visitor sessions matching the query

PagePathsSparklineQuery.description: Query parameters for batch page paths sparkline endpoint

visits.description: Number of visits from this referrer

percentage.description: Percentage of transitions from the source page that go to this destination

transition_count.description: Number of times this transition occurred

PageVisitorSession.description: Individual visitor session that viewed a specific page

is_entry.description: Whether this was the entry page for the session

is_exit.description: Whether this was the exit page for the session

time_on_page.description: Time spent on this page in seconds

has_more.description: Whether there are more entities available

next_token.description: Continuation token for next page (S3, etc.)

Pagination.description:
SDK pagination cursor. We use opaque page numbers internally but
expose `count`/`next`/`prev` the way `@vercel/sandbox` expects.

PasswordProtectionConfig.description:
Password protection configuration

When enabled, the proxy shows an HTML password form before allowing access.
After the user enters the correct password, an HMAC-signed cookie is set
so subsequent requests pass through without re-entering the password.

passwordHash.description: The bcrypt-hashed password (never stored or returned in plaintext)

PeerEntry.description:
Wire-format peer entry. Matches `temps_network::config::Peer` but
uses strings on the wire to keep the API stable across underlying
type evolution.

node_id.description:
Stable v5 UUID derived from the database node id. Workers use
this as the kernel-layer identifier when calling
`NetworkManager::reconcile_peers`.

underlay_address.description:
Address the local node should use to reach this peer over the
underlay (private VPC IP for same-DC, public IP for cross-DC).

oneOf.description:
Caller's own allocation, or `null` if multi-host networking has
not been enabled for this node yet.

peers.description: All other nodes with a `compute_cidr` set, excluding the caller.

id.description: DB id, absent for orphan (S3-scan) backups.

location.description: Resolved S3 location the orchestrator will actually use.

location_was_resolved.description:
True when the original row's `s3_location` was empty and we resolved
a location by probing S3. The UI shows this as a warning.

origin_service_name.description: Service that originally produced the backup, if known.

platforms.description: List of supported platforms in "os/arch" format (e.g., ["linux/amd64"])

PluginManifest.description: The complete plugin manifest — the handshake contract.

description.description: Short description of what the plugin does

events.description:
Platform event types the plugin subscribes to.

When specified, Temps will POST matching events to the plugin's
`/_events` endpoint. Uses dot-notation event names matching the
webhook event types (e.g., "deployment.succeeded", "project.created").

Available events:
- `deployment.created`, `deployment.succeeded`, `deployment.failed`,
  `deployment.cancelled`, `deployment.ready`
- `project.created`, `project.deleted`
- `domain.created`, `domain.provisioned`

health_path.description: Health check endpoint path (relative to plugin root)

nav.description: Navigation entries for the UI sidebar

requires_db.description: Whether the plugin needs database access

oneOf.description: UI bundle manifest (if the plugin has a UI)

host_port.description: Host port (optional - can be assigned dynamically)

is_primary.description: Whether this is the primary HTTP port

archive_backlog.description: Number of `archive_status/*.ready` files — un-shipped WAL segments.

archive_command.description:
The literal `archive_command` setting. May be empty or `/bin/true`
when archiving is effectively disabled despite `archive_mode = on`.

oldest_wal_age_secs.description: Age of the oldest WAL file in `pg_wal/` (seconds).

pg_wal_bytes.description: Total size of files under `pg_wal/`, from `pg_ls_waldir()`.

warnings.description: Computed warnings, ordered by severity (critical first).

oneOf.description: Configuration for Dockerfile preset
Configuration for Nixpacks preset (uses nixpacks.toml, no params needed)
Configuration for static site presets (Vite, Next.js, etc.)

PresetConfigSchema.description:
Union type for preset configurations
Use the appropriate configuration type based on your preset

compose_files.description: Compose file paths found in the repository (only for docker-compose preset)

exposed_port.description: Default exposed port for this preset

path.description: Path where preset was detected (empty for root)

default_port.description: Default port the application listens on (None for static sites)

description.description: Description of what this preset does

slug.description: Unique identifier slug for the preset

PreviewGatewaySettings.description:
Workspace preview gateway settings.

The preview gateway is a single shared Docker container that lives on the
`temps-sandbox-net` network and routes requests to workspace sandbox dev
servers based on the `Host` header (`ws-<sid>-<port>.<preview_domain>`).
`temps serve` reconciles this container on startup; these settings let an
operator override the image, host port, and auto-upgrade behavior.

auto_upgrade.description:
When true (default), the supervisor will pull and apply the image
pinned in the Temps binary on every startup. When false, the
currently-running image is left alone — operators upgrade manually
from the settings UI.

host_port.description:
Host port to publish the gateway on (always bound to 127.0.0.1).
Pingora forwards `ws-*` traffic to this port after authenticating.

image.description:
Docker image reference for the gateway. Pinned per Temps release.
Operators can override this to test a custom build.

shared_secret.description:
Shared secret the host-side Pingora sends on every forwarded preview
request via `X-Temps-Preview-Token`; the gateway rejects requests
without it. Auto-generated on first boot, persisted in DB so the
secret is stable across `temps serve` restarts regardless of cwd,
`TEMPS_DATA_DIR`, or data-dir changes. MUST be masked (`***`) in any
API response — never expose it over HTTP.

default_image.description:
The compile-time default image — exposed so the UI can offer a
"Reset to default" link without round-tripping.

ProblemDetails.description:
Representation of a Problem error to return to the client.
Follows RFC 7807 - Problem Details for HTTP APIs

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem
detail.example: The server encountered an unexpected condition

instance.description: A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem type

type.description: A URI reference that identifies the problem type

example.title: Internal Server Error
example.detail: The server encountered an unexpected condition

is_web_app.description: Whether this is a web application

ProjectDashboardAnalytics.description: Analytics data for a single project in the dashboard batch response

previous_unique_visitors.description: Unique visitor count in the previous period (same duration, shifted back)

trend_percentage.description:
Percentage change from previous period (positive = growth, negative = decline)
Null when previous period had zero visitors (no baseline to compare)

unique_visitors.description: Unique visitor count in the current time range

ProjectHealthSummary.description: Health summary for a single project (last 1 hour)

ProjectMonitorHealth.description: Health summary for a single project based on its production monitors

composeFiles.description: Compose file paths found in the repository (only for docker-compose preset)

exposedPort.description: Default exposed port for this preset (e.g., 3000 for Next.js, 8000 for FastAPI)

attack_mode.description: Attack mode - when enabled, requires CAPTCHA verification for all project environments

enable_preview_environments.description: Enable automatic preview environment creation for each branch

git_url.description: Git clone URL for the repository (used for public repos without a provider connection)

gitlab_webhook_id.description:
GitLab webhook ID installed on the connected repository.
`null` when no GitLab webhook is installed (not connected to GitLab,
or webhook was removed / never created).

preset_config.description: Preset-specific configuration (Dockerfile path, build context, etc.)

preview_envs_idle_timeout_seconds.description: Idle timeout (seconds) for on-demand preview environments.

preview_envs_on_demand.description:
When true, newly-created preview environments default to on-demand mode
(containers stop after the configured idle timeout to save resources).

preview_envs_wake_timeout_seconds.description: Wake timeout (seconds) for on-demand preview environments.

source_type.description: Source type for deployments (git, docker_image, or static_files)

ProjectSecretResponse.description:
Project secret metadata. There is deliberately no `value` field — secret
plaintext is never returned after creation. Callers that need the value
must read it from the mounted file inside the container.

projects.description: Health summaries keyed by project ID

target_environment_id.description: Target environment ID to promote the deployment to

PropertyBreakdownQuery.description: Query parameters for property breakdown (group by column)

end_date.description: End date for the query range

filter_browser.description: Filter by browser name (for browser version drill-downs)

filter_channel.description: Filter by channel name (for channel -> referrer drill-downs)

filter_country.description: Filter by country (for region/city drill-downs). Requires geolocation join.

filter_os.description: Filter by operating system name (for OS version drill-downs)

filter_referrer.description: Filter by referrer hostname (for referrer -> pages drill-downs)

filter_region.description: Filter by region (for city drill-downs). Requires geolocation join.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 20, max: 100)

start_date.description: Start date for the query range

PropertyTimelineQuery.description: Query parameters for property timeline (group by column over time)

end_date.description: End date for the query range

start_date.description: Start date for the query range

ProviderCatalogDto.description: One catalog entry rendered for the settings UI.

credential_saved.description:
True when a credential is currently saved for this provider in the
settings JSON. Lets the UI render "Configured" badges without the
frontend having to inspect the encrypted blob.

current_auth_type.description:
Currently saved auth flavor id (when `credential_saved` is true).
`None` when no credential is saved yet.

default_model.description:
Currently saved default model id for this provider, if one was
picked. `None` means "use the CLI's own default" — the UI renders
that as "Use provider default".

models.description:
Model ids this provider accepts, in display order. The first entry is
the recommended default. Empty when the provider doesn't expose model
selection (e.g. OpenCode), which the UI uses to hide the dropdown.

default_provider.description:
Active provider id from `agent_sandbox.default_provider`. The settings
UI uses this to highlight which card is the active one.

ProviderConfig.description:
Provider-specific integration settings persisted in
`revenue_integrations.config`.

The tag is the lowercase provider name, so adding a new provider
means adding a new variant and the existing rows are untouched.
Old rows (pre-config) and rows with `NULL` config are treated as
"accept all events, no filtering" via [`ProviderConfig::default_for`].

credential_saved.description:
True if a credential is stored for this provider. The encrypted blob
is never returned over HTTP.

api_key_masked.description: Masked API key (only last 4 chars visible)

branch.description: Branch name where presets were detected

before.description: Delete all logs before this timestamp (ISO 8601)

PushImageRequest.description: Request to push an external image

PushedExternalImageResponse.description:
Response for in-memory external image operations (legacy push flow).

Renamed to avoid shadowing the richer database-backed `ExternalImageResponse`
in `handlers/remote_deployments.rs`. The two types serve different routes
(`/images` ephemeral push vs `/external-images` registered images).

limit.description: Maximum number of rows to return

returned_count.description: Number of rows returned in this response

total_count.description: Total number of rows matching the query (before limit/offset)

RateLimitConfig.description: Rate limiting configuration (subset of global RateLimitSettings)

blacklistIps.description: Blacklist specific IPs for this project/environment

whitelistIps.description: Whitelist specific IPs for this project/environment

RecentActivityQuery.description: Query parameters for recent activity endpoint

limit.description: Max number of events to return (default: 50, max: 100)

since_id.description: Return events with ID greater than this (for cursor-based polling)

RecentActivityResponse.description: Response for recent activity events endpoint

offset.description: Number of results to skip for pagination (defaults to 0)

status.description: Filter by HTTP status code (exact match)

oneOf.description: Recover to a specific timestamp.
Recover to a specific transaction id (Postgres).
Recover to a specific log sequence number (Postgres).
Recover to a named restore point created via `pg_create_restore_point` (Postgres).

RecoveryTarget.description:
Engine-specific recovery target for PITR.

Postgres honors all variants; Redis/Mongo/S3 will likely reject non-Time
variants or define their own semantics when they grow PITR support.

edge_public_key.description: X25519 public key for ECIES certificate encryption (base64-encoded, edge nodes only)

join_token.description: Join token to authorize this registration (must match the token generated in Settings)

token.description: Registration token (plaintext, will be hashed before storage)

hook_id.description: The new GitLab hook ID that was installed.

loaded.description: Number of plugins successfully loaded after reload

git_provider_connection_id.description: ID of the git provider connection this repository was synced from.

RepositorySyncStartedResponse.description:
Returned by `POST /git-connections/{id}/sync` to acknowledge that a
sync has been kicked off in the background. Clients should poll the
connection's `syncing` and `synced_repository_count` fields to track
progress rather than waiting on this response.

ResolvedEnvVarResponse.description:
One entry in the computed env-var view that merges manual and integration
sources and tags each result with its origin. `value_preview` is always
masked — plaintext must be fetched per-key via the existing reveal endpoint,
which is audit-logged.

environments.description:
Environments this var applies to. For integration-sourced vars this
reflects every environment of the project (integrations are global).

include_in_preview.description:
Whether the var would be auto-applied to preview environments.
Integration vars always surface in preview; manual vars follow the flag.

value_preview.description: Masked or truncated preview. Never the raw value.

oneOf.description:
Manually-defined env var. If `overrides_service` is set, this key would
otherwise have been supplied by an integration — the UI should show the
integration icon plus an "overridden" indicator.
Supplied by a linked external service (Postgres, Redis, S3, etc.).

ResolvedEnvVarSource.description:
Where a resolved env var comes from. Integration-sourced vars may be
"shadowed" by a manual entry with the same key, in which case the response
carries `Manual` with `overrides_service` populated so the UI can still show
the integration icon.

ResourceCounts.description: Quick count of resources involved in the migration

ResourceInfo.description: Resource attributes extracted from OTel resource descriptors.

ResourceLimitApplyResult.description:
Per-container outcome of a live `docker update` call. Surfaced from the
PATCH /resources endpoint so the UI can tell the operator whether the
new caps are already in effect or whether they only apply on next
recreate (e.g., container was missing).

outcome.description:
One of:
- "applied"  — Docker accepted the update; caps are live now.
- "missing"  — container does not exist; caps stored, will apply on next start.
- "stopped"  — container exists but isn't running; Docker still
  accepts the update (the new caps apply on next start).
- "failed"   — `docker update` returned an error (see `error`).

applied.description: Per-container result of trying to apply the limits live.

limits.description: The limits that were persisted to the encrypted config.

ResourcesBody.description:
Nested `resources: { memory, vcpus }` as sent by `@vercel/sandbox`.
`memory` is in MB, `vcpus` is fractional CPU count.

RestoreCapabilities.description:
Capabilities a service exposes for the generic restore framework.

Each engine overrides `ExternalService::restore_capabilities` to declare
what it supports. The handler layer uses this to validate requests and
the UI uses it to conditionally show options (e.g., PITR picker).

earliest_pitr_time.description:
Earliest recoverable timestamp, if `pitr` is true. Derived from
engine-specific archive metadata (e.g., `pg_stat_archiver`).

latest_pitr_time.description: Latest recoverable timestamp, if `pitr` is true.

pitr.description:
Point-in-time recovery using engine-specific continuous archives
(WAL for Postgres, AOF for Redis, oplog for MongoDB, object versions for S3).

restore_in_place.description: Restore a backup onto the same running service (destructive).

restore_to_new_service.description: Restore a backup into a freshly provisioned service.

suggested_new_service_name.description:
Suggested name for the new service when creating a clone. Safe to
pre-fill into the UI dialog; the user can edit before submitting.

RestorePlan.description:
Preview of a restore operation. Answers "what will happen if I click
start?" with engine-level specificity so the user can confirm before
committing to a destructive action.

destructive.description: Whether any step overwrites existing data on the target service.

errors.description: Blocking problems. The UI disables the Start button when non-empty.

mode.description: Echo of the requested mode for the UI.

steps.description: Ordered list of human-readable actions the orchestrator will take.

strategy.description:
How the restore will be performed: "walg_restore", "pg_dump_restore",
or "unsupported".

target_service.description: Service we'll operate on (or provision a sibling of).

warnings.description:
Non-blocking caveats the user should see (cross-service, empty
location that will be auto-resolved, missing engine metadata, ...).

oneOf.description: Restore the backup onto the existing service (destructive).
Provision a new service and restore into it.

name.description:
Name for the new service. Orchestrator auto-suggests
`{source}-restore-{yyyymmdd-hhmm}` if caller omits, but we require
an explicit value at the API boundary.

oneOf.description: Point-in-time recovery. Only valid on WAL-G backups (Postgres).

to_new_service.description: Whether PITR restores in place or creates a new service.

RestoreRequestMode.description:
What the caller wants to do. Mirrors `externalsvc::RestoreMode` but
flattened for JSON over the wire.

RetryClusterRequest.description: Request body for retrying a failed cluster initialization.

members.description:
Cluster member specifications (same format as create).
If omitted, the original member configuration is reconstructed from
the preserved service_members records.

RiskLevel.description: Risk level for a migration step

s3_source_id.description: ID of the S3 source to store the backup. If omitted, the current default S3 source is used.

S3ConnectionTestResponse.description: Response body for an S3 connection test.

message.description: Human-readable message (success confirmation or error detail).

ok.description: Whether the connection and credentials worked.

S3CredentialsResponse.description: S3 credentials distributed to agents for backup/restore operations.

SandboxInner.description:
Inner `sandbox` object in `@vercel/sandbox` responses. Strict shape —
the SDK's zod validator rejects missing required fields.

requestedAt.description: Creation time as Unix epoch milliseconds.

SandboxResponse.description:
`@vercel/sandbox` wraps every single-sandbox response as
`{ sandbox: {...}, routes: [...] }`. The SDK reads both.

SandboxRoute.description:
A single preview route, one per declared port. We don't know ports
upfront, so we surface an empty array by default — SDK clients use
their own port when calling `sandbox.domain(port)`.

token.description:
The OAuth token from `claude setup-token` or an API key.
Will be encrypted before storage.

auth_type.description: Auth flavor id (must match one of the provider's catalog entries).

credential.description:
Plaintext credential body (API key, OAuth token, or full config file
contents). Encrypted with `EncryptionService` before being persisted
inside the `agent_sandbox.providers` JSON map.

ScheduleRunEntry.description:
A single run-history entry for the schedule detail page (deliverable 1).

Combines one `backups` row with the most-recent `backup_jobs` row for that
backup via a lateral JOIN.  Fields from `backup_jobs` are `None` for legacy
backup rows that pre-date ADR-014.

attempts.description: Number of claim-and-run attempts so far. `None` for legacy rows.

current_step.description:
Last completed step reported by the engine (e.g. `"upload"`).
`None` when no step has been persisted yet.

finished_at.description: When the backup finished, if known.

job_id.description: Most recent `backup_jobs.id` for this backup. `None` for legacy rows.

s3_location.description: S3 object key or URL where the backup data lives.

ServiceResourceLimits.description:
Optional cgroup resource limits applied to a service container.

All fields are `Option<i64>`: `None` means "no limit" (the kernel default),
matching Docker's behavior when the corresponding `HostConfig` field is
left at zero. Operators opt in to limits explicitly through the
`PATCH /external-services/{id}/resources` endpoint or by writing the
`resources` block into `ServiceConfig::parameters` at create time.

These map directly onto bollard fields:
- `memory_mb`     → `HostConfig.memory`        (bytes)
- `memory_swap_mb`→ `HostConfig.memory_swap`   (bytes; ≥ memory)
- `nano_cpus`     → `HostConfig.nano_cpus`     (1e9 = 1 full CPU)
- `cpu_shares`    → `HostConfig.cpu_shares`    (relative weight, default 1024)

IMPORTANT: enabling hard memory limits causes the kernel OOM killer to
terminate the container when the working set exceeds the limit. The
container will restart (RestartPolicy::ALWAYS) but in-flight queries
fail. Surface this clearly in any UI that lets users set limits.

cpu_shares.description: Relative CPU weight (default 1024). Only used when `nano_cpus` is None.

memory_mb.description: Hard memory limit in MiB. None = unlimited.

memory_swap_mb.description:
Memory + swap limit in MiB. None = unlimited.
MUST be >= memory_mb when both are set; Docker rejects the request otherwise.
Set equal to `memory_mb` to disable swap entirely.

nano_cpus.description: CPU quota in nano-cpus. 1_000_000_000 = 1 full CPU core. None = unlimited.

SourceBody.description:
Initial content to seed into the sandbox work dir. Mirrors the
`@vercel/sandbox` `source` option. `type` is one of:
- `git` — clone `url`; optionally check out `revision`
- `tarball` — download `url` (must be tar or tar.gz) and extract

For private git repos, pass credentials one of two ways:
1. **Inline (SDK-compatible):** `username` + `password`. GitHub
   tokens use `username: "x-access-token"`.
2. **Stored connection (temps-native):** `git_connection_id`
   references a row in the caller's git provider connections. Temps
   resolves the token server-side and injects it safely.

`git_connection_id` is mutually exclusive with `username`/`password`.

- `DockerImage`: Pre-built Docker image from external registry
- `StaticFiles`: Pre-built static files uploaded as a bundle
- `Manual`: Flexible type that accepts any deployment method

UpdateAiProviderRequest.description:
Body for `PATCH /settings/ai-providers/{provider_id}` — updates
provider-scoped settings (just the default model for now) without
touching the credential. Keeping credentials out of this shape means
the UI can auto-save model changes on select, without forcing the user
to re-paste their token or config file.
Name-spaced schema name avoids an OpenAPI collision with
`temps-notifications::UpdateProviderRequest`, which has different fields.
Both are exposed as `utoipa::ToSchema`; without the override the merged
OpenAPI doc would silently shadow one struct with the other and break
generated CLI/web clients.

default_model.description:

value so the CLI falls back to its own default.

UpdateBackupScheduleRequest.description:
Request body for updating an existing backup schedule via `PATCH /api/backups/schedules/{id}`.

All fields are optional; only present fields are updated. Absent fields
leave the corresponding column unchanged.

description.description: New human-readable description. Pass an empty string `""` to clear.

enabled.description: Enable or disable the schedule. Skipped when `None`.

include_control_plane.description: Toggle whether the control-plane backup is produced on every run.

max_runtime_secs.description:
Per-schedule wall-clock timeout override (seconds).

- `None` (field absent) — leave current value unchanged
- `Some(None)` (field present, JSON `null`) — clear override; fall back to engine default
- `Some(Some(n))` — set to `n` seconds (must be >= 60)

name.description: New schedule name. Skipped when `None`. Must not be empty if provided.

retention_period.description: Days to retain backups produced by this schedule. Must be >= 1.

schedule_expression.description: New cron expression. When changed, `next_run` is recomputed.

tags.description: Replace the full tag list. Skipped when `None`.

target_all_services.description:
Toggle between "back up every database" (`true`) and "back up only
the explicit list" (`false`). When set to `true`, the server clears
the explicit membership rows for this schedule.

Typed provider configuration. Setting `config` to `null` clears
